chunk_summary: This section outlines macronutrient distribution targets for children using Dietary Reference Intakes (DRI), emphasizing age-specific ranges for fat, carbohydrates, and protein to support growth, prevent obesity, and adapt to cultural food practices.
learning_objectives: Understand the DRI Acceptable Macronutrient Distribution Range (AMDR) for children aged 1–3 and 4–18 years.
Learn strategies to adjust visible fat intake in Indian diets for wasting or obesity management.
Recognize the importance of macronutrient quality and specialist-led care in complex cases like fat malabsorption.
key_facts: Age 1–3 years: Fat 30–40%, Carbohydrate 45–65%, Protein 5–20% of energy.
Age 4–18 years: Fat 25–35%, Carbohydrate 45–65%, Protein 10–30% of energy.
Saturated fat should remain ≤10% of energy in overweight/obesity with dyslipidemia risk.
Visible fat adjustments (e.g., oils, ghee) can increase energy density without increasing meal volume.
Avoid industrial trans fats and prioritize unsaturated household oils for healthier fat sources.
reference_quote: Use the DRI Acceptable Macronutrient Distribution Range (AMDR) as a bedside framework, not as a rigid diet.
document_key_topics: Strategies for managing wasting and obesity in children
